description: Pattern matching rules optimized for Claude models
weaknesses: No real-time web search by default

system_level_structuring.description: Clear role, context, and execution environment

patterns.name: role_assignment
patterns.regex: ^(you are|act as|assume you|take on the role of|imagine you are)
patterns.description: Claude responds better with explicit role

format_blueprints.description: Concrete specification of expected output format

patterns.name: structure_detail
patterns.description: Detail about expected structure via labels or numbered lists

chain_of_thought.description: Explicit request for reasoning or multi-step analysis

patterns.name: step_by_step
patterns.description: Claude excels at multi-step reasoning and structured analysis

patterns.name: before_generating
patterns.description: Instruction to reason or outline before responding

patterns.name: explain_approach
patterns.description: Request to explain approach or cover specific analytic areas

constraint_clarity.description: Clear boundaries — what to include, exclude, and how long

patterns.name: avoid_list
patterns.regex: (avoid:|do not:|don't include|exclude|without jargon|no pseudocode|no platitudes|no filler|not more than|constraints:)
patterns.description: Explicit things to avoid or constraint block label

multimodal_grounding.description: Using Claude's vision capabilities or rich document grounding

patterns.name: image_reference
patterns.regex: (attached image|this image|this screenshot|this diagram|this wireframe|this photo|this picture|see the image|the image shows|the photo)
patterns.description: Explicitly referencing an attached image or visual

penalty_patterns.name: conversational_filler
penalty_patterns.regex: (please|could you|would you|thank you in advance|kindly|if possible)
penalty_patterns.description: Remove politeness filler - Claude prefers directness

penalty_patterns.name: knowledge_cutoff_bypass
penalty_patterns.description: For current info, explicitly enable web search

penalty_patterns.name: image_generation_request
penalty_patterns.description: Claude cannot generate images - describe instead

penalty_patterns.name: over_engineering
penalty_patterns.description: Claude prefers focused, not exhaustive responses

bonus_patterns.name: long_context_utilization
bonus_patterns.description: Claude can handle long context well

interrogation_questions.question: What's your professional background or expertise level for this task?
interrogation_questions.alternative: What role should I take? (e.g., Senior Engineer, Product Manager, Analyst)

interrogation_questions.question: What's your tech stack, use case, or operating environment?
interrogation_questions.alternative: What context should I consider for this problem?

interrogation_questions.question: Should I explain my reasoning before giving the final answer?
interrogation_questions.alternative: Would you like me to think step-by-step first?

interrogation_questions.question: Are there any constraints I should respect? (length, style, scope, taboos)
interrogation_questions.alternative: What should I avoid or not include?

interrogation_questions.question: How should I structure the response? (format, sections, output type)
interrogation_questions.alternative: Can you show an example of the output structure you want?

interrogation_questions.question: Do you have a wireframe, diagram, or screenshot that would help clarify this?
interrogation_questions.alternative: Would a visual reference improve accuracy?

tips: Claude excels at long-form reasoning - provide detailed context
Constitutional AI training means Claude considers ethics - leverage this
Explicit constraints are respected - use them liberally
Claude can analyze images - attach wireframes or screenshots
For current information, explicitly ask to search or mention cutoff

example_prompts.good: You are an AI expert. Explain artificial intelligence to a 15-year-old, using simple examples. Keep it under 200 words. Format: Definition, Key concepts, Real-world impact.
example_prompts.excellent:
You are a senior computer science teacher with 10 years experience teaching teenagers.

Explain artificial intelligence to a bright 15-year-old student who knows basic math.

Structure:
1. Definition (1-2 sentences)
2. How it works (2-3 sentences with analogy)
3. Real-world examples (3-4 specific examples)
4. Why it matters (1-2 sentences)

Tone: Friendly, encouraging, avoids jargon
Length: 150-200 words
Format: Numbered sections
Avoid: Technical terms without explanation, boring examples
